mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon. This reflects a healthy consumption environment supporting equity valuations.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over a 5-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC is consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate, specifically a rise of 0.5 percentage points or more over three months, signals a deteriorating labor market. This leads to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, thus negatively impacting S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: If, following a trigger event, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive or not statistically different from zero in more than 40% of observed instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high (e.g., above 230) and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is simultaneously low (e.g., below 0.2%), it indicates an accommodative monetary policy despite inflationary pressures. This can lead to asset price bubbles and eventual corrections, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns as policy makers are forced to tighten.
regime_hint: inflationary environment with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: If, when both CPIAUCSL is above 230 and FEDFUNDS is below 0.2, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly positive or flat over a 3-year period, indicating no market correction.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, indicated by robust GDP expansion, positive housing starts (HOUST) signal increasing investment and consumer confidence. This leads to short-term positive S&P 500 returns as market participants anticipate continued growth and strong corporate performance.
failure_condition: If, during periods where GDPC1 growth is above its long-term average and HOUST is above 800, the 5-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic supply capacity. This supports corporate earnings and translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, as productive capacity drives economic expansion.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward S&P 500 returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with the year-over-year growth rate of INDPRO over a 7-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), specifically a drop of 100,000 persons or more month-over-month, signals a weakening labor market. This dampens consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate an economic slowdown.
failure_condition: If, after a PAYEMS decline of over 100,000, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of the triggered events over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is very low (e.g., below 0.2%) and CPIAUCSL shows signs of accelerating inflation (e.g., MoM > 0.3%), it suggests the central bank is behind the curve. This can lead to market uncertainty and short-term negative S&P 500 reactions as investors price in future aggressive tightening.
failure_condition: If, under these specific conditions, the 5-day forward S&P 500 returns are consistently positive or flat, indicating no immediate negative market reaction to the perceived policy lag over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary environment, a significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a severe contraction in construction and investment. This further exacerbates economic weakness and leads to prolonged negative S&P 500 returns, reflecting broad-based economic distress.
failure_condition: If, during officially declared recessionary periods, a 10% decline in HOUST over three months does not precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 70% of occurrences over multiple business cycles.
